name: Windows Desktop Client Reverse Engineering and Trust Abuse
description: Attack tree for reversing a Windows desktop client, bypassing trust controls, and abusing local or backend privileges.
focus_areas: Binary reverse engineering and runtime tracing
Local secret extraction and hidden functionality discovery
prompt_hints: Offer debugger, disassembler, ETW, Frida, and API-hooking level detail.
Prefer concrete trust boundaries, privileged helpers, local storage artifacts, and protocol surfaces.
root_objective: Reverse engineer a Windows desktop client to bypass local trust controls and abuse backend functionality

nodes.title: Abuse Backend and Local Trust via Reversed Windows Client
nodes.description: Reverse engineer the Windows desktop client, defeat trust controls, and use recovered functionality or secrets to access unauthorized actions.
nodes.required_tools: IDA Pro, Ghidra, dnSpy, x64dbg, Frida

nodes.title: Map Client Internals and Protocol Logic
nodes.description: Recover the desktop client's internal workflows, protocol handlers, and trust decisions to understand how it reaches privileged backend actions.

nodes.title: Recover API and Auth Workflow from Binary
nodes.description: Disassemble or decompile the client to recover endpoint lists, protocol models, token lifecycles, and hidden feature toggles.

nodes.title: Trace Privileged Helper Service or IPC Surface
nodes.description: Instrument named pipes, COM, RPC, or local TCP control channels to identify operations the client can request from elevated helper components.
nodes.attack_surface: Named pipes, COM, RPC, localhost services

nodes.title: Defeat Client Trust Controls
nodes.description: Bypass local integrity, certificate, or anti-debug controls that prevent interception, tampering, or runtime instrumentation.

nodes.title: Patch or Hook Integrity and Anti-Debug Checks
nodes.description: Identify anti-debug, checksum, or self-integrity paths and neutralize them with binary patches or runtime hooks to keep instrumentation stable.

nodes.title: Bypass TLS Pinning or Mutual Trust Validation
nodes.description: Hook certificate validation, pinning, or trust-store checks so the client accepts operator-controlled interception infrastructure.

nodes.title: Abuse Hidden Functionality or Privileged Boundaries
nodes.description: Use reversed features, undocumented routes, or helper services to trigger actions the product assumed were unreachable or trusted.
nodes.attack_surface: Hidden client functions and helper boundary

nodes.title: Invoke Hidden Admin or Debug Operations
nodes.description: Replay undocumented client verbs, feature flags, or internal API routes discovered during reversing to access unsupported or privileged backend actions.

nodes.title: Exploit Helper Service Command Validation Gap
nodes.description: Send crafted IPC or RPC requests that the elevated helper trusts because the original client was assumed to be honest.
nodes.attack_surface: Named pipe, COM, RPC, or local admin service

nodes.title: Extract Local Secrets and Session Material
nodes.description: Harvest locally cached secrets, tokens, and state data that allow persistent access or backend replay without continued reversing.

nodes.title: Recover Tokens from SQLite, Config, or DPAPI-Protected Stores
nodes.description: Parse the client's local databases, configuration stores, and protected blobs to recover reusable tokens, refresh credentials, or signing keys.
nodes.required_tools: sqlite3, Mimikatz DPAPI modules, custom scripts
